revision.text: #REDIRECT [[湯の川温泉 (北海道)]]

{{河川情報| 名称=湯川| 画像=| 水系=[[千曲川|千曲川水系]]（[[信濃川|信濃川水系]]）| 延長=---| 標高=---| 流量=---| 流域面積=---| 水源=[[浅間山]] [[碓氷峠]]| 河口=[[千曲川]]（[[信濃川]]）| 流域=[[長野県]] }} '''湯川'''（ゆかわ）は、[[長野県]]を流れる[[千曲川]]（[[信濃川]]）の支流の1つである。
== 概要 == [[浅間山]]の[[碓氷峠]]から流れ、[[千曲川]]（[[信濃川]]）に流れ込む。上流には、[[湯川ダム]]がある。
== 関連項目 == *[[千曲川]] *[[浅間山]] *[[湯川ダム]]
[[Category:長野県の河川|ゆかわ]]

{{河川情報 |名称=湯川 |画像= |水系等級= |水系=[[信濃川]] |種別= |延長=-- |標高=-- |流量=-- |流域面積=-- |水源=[[浅間山]] |河口=[[千曲川]]<small>（[[信濃川]]）</small> |流域=[[長野県]] }} '''湯川'''（ゆかわ）は、[[長野県]]の[[軽井沢]]および佐久地域を流れる[[河川]]。[[千曲川]]（[[信濃川]]）の支流。
== 地理 == 長野県[[北佐久郡]][[軽井沢町]]北部の[[浅間山]]付近に源を発し、[[碓氷峠]]からの河川を合わせ、[[佐久市]]鳴瀬で[[千曲川]]（[[信濃川]]）に[[合流]]する。上流には、[[湯川ダム]]がある。
[[Category:長野県の河川|ゆかわ]]

== 地理 == 長野県[[北佐久郡]][[軽井沢町]]北部の[[浅間山]]付近に源を発し、[[碓氷峠]]からの河川を合わせ、[[佐久市]]鳴瀬で千曲川（信濃川）に[[合流]]する。上流には、[[湯川ダム]]がある。

{{Otheruses|長野県の河川|その他の湯川|湯川 (曖昧さ回避)}} {{Infobox 河川 |名称=湯川 |画像=[[ファイル:Yukawa Riv.JPG|300px|2010年5月4日撮影]] |画像説明=長倉公園付近を流れる湯川（背後は[[浅間山]]） |水系等級= |水系=[[信濃川]] |種別=[[一級河川]] |延長=-- |標高=-- |流量=-- |流域面積=-- |水源=[[浅間山]] |河口=[[信濃川|千曲川]]<small>（[[信濃川]]）</small> |流域={{JPN}}[[長野県]] |脚注= |出典= }} '''湯川'''（ゆかわ）は、[[長野県]]の[[軽井沢]]および佐久地域を流れる[[河川]]。[[信濃川|千曲川]]（信濃川）の支流。
== 地理 == 長野県[[北佐久郡]][[軽井沢町]]北部の[[浅間山]]付近に源を発し、[[碓氷峠]]からの河川を合わせ、[[佐久市]]鳴瀬で千曲川（信濃川）に[[合流]]する。上流には、[[湯川ダム]]がある。
== 行政指定 == 指定内容は、以下のとおり<ref>1965年（昭和40年）[[3月24日]]、[[政令]]第43号「河川法第四条第一項の水系及び一級河川を指定する政令」</ref>。 * 河川指定:一級河川（[[1965年]]指定） * 流端 ** 上流端:長野県北佐久郡軽井沢町大字長倉字獅子岩地籍地先 ** 下流端:信濃川への合流点 * 水系指定:[[一級水系]]信濃川水系（1965年指定）
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

{{Infobox 河川 |名称=湯川 |画像=[[ファイル:Yukawa Riv.JPG|250px|2010年5月4日撮影]] |画像説明=長倉公園付近を流れる湯川（背後は[[浅間山]]） |水系等級=[[一級水系]] |水系=[[信濃川]] |種別=[[一級河川]] |延長= |標高= |流量= |流域面積= |水源=[[浅間山]] |河口=[[信濃川|千曲川]] |流路=[[長野県]][[北佐久郡]][[軽井沢町]]・[[御代田町]]・[[佐久市]] |流域=長野県北佐久郡軽井沢町・御代田町・佐久市 |脚注= |出典= }} '''湯川'''（ゆかわ）は、[[長野県]][[北佐久郡]][[軽井沢町]]・[[御代田町]]、[[佐久市]]を流れる[[川]]で、[[信濃川]][[水系]]の[[一級河川]]。
== 地理 == 長野県[[北佐久郡]][[軽井沢町]]北部の[[浅間山]]付近に源を発し、[[白糸の滝 (長野県)|白糸の滝]]や[[千ヶ滝 (軽井沢町)|千ヶ滝]]、[[碓氷峠]]などからの河川を合わせ、[[御代田町]]を経て[[佐久市]]鳴瀬で千曲川（長野県における信濃川の呼称）に[[合流]]する。御代田町の湯川渓谷には長野[[都道府県営ダム|県営]]の[[治水ダム]]である[[湯川ダム]]がある。
<gallery> File:Karuizawa shiraito-no-taki04s3872.jpg|白糸の滝 File:Sengataki Waterfall (Karuizawa).jpg|千ヶ滝 File:Yu River view from Karuizawa-ohashi bridge 2.jpg|[[軽井沢大橋]]より望む湯川渓谷 File:Yukawa Dam and lake.jpg|湯川ダム </gallery>
== 行政指定 == 指定内容は、以下のとおり<ref>1965年（昭和40年）[[3月24日]]、[[政令]]第43号「河川法第四条第一項の水系及び一級河川を指定する政令」</ref>。 * 河川指定:一級河川（[[1965年]]指定） * 流端 ** 上流端:長野県北佐久郡軽井沢町大字長倉字獅子岩地籍地先 ** 下流端:信濃川への合流点 * 水系指定:[[一級水系]]信濃川水系（1965年指定）

{{Infobox 河川 |名称=湯川 |画像=[[ファイル:Yukawa Riv.JPG|250px|2010年5月4日撮影]] |画像説明=長倉公園付近を流れる湯川（背後は[[浅間山]]） {{Maplink2|zoom=10|frame=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=292|frame-height=300|frame-latitude=36.27|frame-longitude=138.52 |type=shape|stroke-color=#0000ff|stroke-width=1|title=湯川 |type2=line|stroke-color2=#0000ff|stroke-width2=1|title2=湯川 |type3=shape|stroke-color3=#777777|stroke-width3=1|id3=Q61041464|title3=千曲川 |text='''青.'''湯川、'''灰.'''信濃川（千曲川） }} |水系等級=[[一級水系]] |水系=[[信濃川]] |種別=[[一級河川]] |延長= |標高= |流量= |流域面積= |水源=[[浅間山]] |河口=[[信濃川]]（千曲川） |流路=[[長野県]][[北佐久郡]][[軽井沢町]]・[[御代田町]]・[[佐久市]] |流域=長野県北佐久郡軽井沢町・御代田町・佐久市 |脚注= |出典= }} '''湯川'''（ゆかわ）は、[[長野県]][[北佐久郡]][[軽井沢町]]・[[御代田町]]、[[佐久市]]を流れる[[川]]で、[[信濃川]][[水系]]の[[一級河川]]。[[江戸時代]]には、鵜川、大川、鼻顔川とも言われた<ref>『中山道第七巻』東京美術1979年9月30日発行全40頁中10頁</ref>。
== 地理 == 長野県[[北佐久郡]][[軽井沢町]]北部の[[浅間山]]付近に源を発し、[[白糸の滝 (長野県)|白糸の滝]]や[[千ヶ滝 (軽井沢町)|千ヶ滝]]、[[碓氷峠]]などからの河川を合わせ、[[御代田町]]を経て[[佐久市]]鳴瀬で千曲川（長野県における信濃川の呼称）に[[合流]]する。御代田町の湯川渓谷には長野[[都道府県営ダム|県営]]の[[治水ダム]]である[[湯川ダム]]がある。
== 行政指定 == 指定内容は、以下のとおり<ref>1965年（昭和40年）[[3月24日]]、[[政令]]第43号「河川法第四条第一項の水系及び一級河川を指定する政令」</ref>。 * 河川指定:一級河川（[[1965年]]指定） * 流端 ** 上流端:長野県北佐久郡軽井沢町大字[[長倉 (軽井沢町)|長倉]]字獅子岩地籍地先 ** 下流端:信濃川への合流点 * 水系指定:[[一級水系]]信濃川水系（1965年指定）
== 参考画像 == <gallery> Karuizawa shiraito-no-taki04s3872.jpg|白糸の滝 Sengataki Waterfall (Karuizawa).jpg|千ヶ滝 Yu River view from Karuizawa-ohashi bridge 2.jpg|[[軽井沢大橋]]より望む湯川渓谷 Yukawa Dam and lake.jpg|湯川ダム File:Hanazura-inari-jinja structure 1.jpg|[[鼻顔稲荷神社]]付近 </gallery>
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox 河川 |名称=湯川 |画像=[[ファイル:Yukawa Riv.JPG|250px|2010年5月4日撮影]] |画像説明=長倉公園付近を流れる湯川（背後は[[浅間山]]） |水系等級=[[一級水系]] |水系=[[信濃川]] |種別=[[一級河川]] |延長= |標高= |流量= |流域面積= |水源=[[浅間山]] |河口=[[信濃川]]（千曲川） |流路=[[長野県]][[北佐久郡]][[軽井沢町]]・[[御代田町]]・[[佐久市]] |流域=長野県北佐久郡軽井沢町・御代田町・佐久市 |地図={{Maplink2|zoom=10|frame=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=292|frame-height=300|frame-latitude=36.27|frame-longitude=138.52 |type=line|stroke-color=#0000ff|stroke-width=3|title=湯川 |type2=line|stroke-color2=#777777|stroke-width2=3|id2=Q734455|title2=信濃川 |text='''青.'''湯川、'''灰.'''信濃川（千曲川） }} |脚注= |出典= }} '''湯川'''（ゆかわ）は、[[長野県]][[北佐久郡]][[軽井沢町]]・[[御代田町]]、[[佐久市]]を流れる[[川]]で、[[信濃川]][[水系]]の[[一級河川]]。[[江戸時代]]には、鵜川、大川、鼻顔川とも言われた<ref>『中山道第七巻』東京美術1979年9月30日発行全40頁中10頁</ref>。
== 地理 == 長野県[[北佐久郡]][[軽井沢町]]北部の[[浅間山]]付近に源を発し、[[白糸の滝 (長野県)|白糸の滝]]や[[千ヶ滝 (軽井沢町)|千ヶ滝]]、[[碓氷峠]]などからの河川を合わせ、[[御代田町]]を経て[[佐久市]]鳴瀬で千曲川（長野県における信濃川の呼称）に[[合流]]する。御代田町の湯川渓谷には長野[[都道府県営ダム|県営]]の[[治水ダム]]である[[湯川ダム]]がある。
== 行政指定 == 指定内容は、以下のとおり<ref>1965年（昭和40年）[[3月24日]]、[[政令]]第43号「河川法第四条第一項の水系及び一級河川を指定する政令」</ref>。 * 河川指定:一級河川（[[1965年]]指定） * 流端 ** 上流端:長野県北佐久郡軽井沢町大字[[長倉 (軽井沢町)|長倉]]字獅子岩地籍地先 ** 下流端:信濃川への合流点 * 水系指定:[[一級水系]]信濃川水系（1965年指定）
== 参考画像 == <gallery> Karuizawa shiraito-no-taki04s3872.jpg|白糸の滝 Sengataki Waterfall (Karuizawa).jpg|千ヶ滝 Yu River view from Karuizawa-ohashi bridge 2.jpg|[[軽井沢大橋]]より望む湯川渓谷 Yukawa Dam and lake.jpg|湯川ダム File:Hanazura-inari-jinja structure 1.jpg|[[鼻顔稲荷神社]]付近 </gallery>
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == {{Commonscat}} * [[軽井沢大橋]] * [[泥川]] {{信濃川水系}} {{デフォルトソート:ゆかわきたさくくん}} [[Category:長野県の河川]] [[Category:信濃川の支流]] [[Category:信濃川水系]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[湯川 (北佐久郡)]] to [[Yugawa River]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Yu River (Kitasaku District, Nagano)]][[ja:湯川 (北佐久郡)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11563522]] Wikidata name: Yu River [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox river | name = Yukawa | image = [[File:Yukawa Riv.JPG|250px|Taken on May 4, 2010]] | image_caption = Yukawa flowing near Nagakura Park (Asama Volcano in the background) | river_system = [[First-class river system]] | water_system = [[:en:Shinano River|Shinano River]] | river_class = [[First-class river]] | length = | source_altitude = | flow_rate = | basin_size = | source = [[Asama Volcano]] | mouth = [[:en:Shinano River|Shinano River]] (Chikuma River) | route = [[Karuizawa Town]], [[Miyota Town]], [[Saku City]], Nagano Prefecture | basin = Karuizawa Town, Miyota Town, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture | map = {{Maplink2|zoom=10|frame=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=292|frame-height=300|frame-latitude=36.27|frame-longitude=138.52 |type=line|stroke-color=#0000ff|stroke-width=3|title=Yukawa |type2=line|stroke-color2=#777777|stroke-width2=3|id2=Q734455|title2=Shinano River |text='''Blue:''' Yukawa, '''Gray:''' Shinano River (Chikuma River) }} | notes = | sources = }}
'''Yukawa''' (湯川) is a [[:en:River|River]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], flowing through [[Karuizawa Town]], [[Miyota Town]], and [[Saku City]]. It is a [[first-class river]] in the [[:en:Shinano River|Shinano River]] system. During the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], it was also known as Uogawa, Ōgawa, and Hanazugawa.<ref>''Nakasendō Volume 7'', Tokyo Bijutsu, September 30, 1979, p. 10.</ref>
== Geography ==
Yukawa originates near the northern part of [[Asama Volcano]] in [[Karuizawa Town]], Nagano Prefecture. It flows through notable locations like {{ill|Shiraito Falls (Nagano)|ja|白糸の滝 (長野県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraito Falls (Nagano)|qq=|1=Shiraito Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580293}}, {{ill|Sengataki Falls (Karuizawa)|ja|千ヶ滝 (軽井沢町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengataki Falls (Karuizawa)|qq=|1=Sengataki Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405218}}, and [[:en:Usui Pass|Usui Pass]], joining the Chikuma River (the local name for the Shinano River) at Naruse in Saku City. In Miyota Town, the Yukawa Valley houses the [[:en:Yukawa Dam|Yukawa Dam]], a prefectural [[flood control dam]] managed by Nagano Prefecture.
== Administrative Designation ==
The river has the following designations:<ref>Cabinet Order No. 43, March 24, 1965: "Ordinance Specifying River Systems and First-Class Rivers Under Article 4, Paragraph 1 of the River Act."</ref> * River designation: First-class river (designated in [[:en:1965|1965]]) * Endpoints: ** Upstream: Nagakura, Karuizawa Town, Nagano Prefecture, at the Shishiiwa land plot ** Downstream: Confluence with the Shinano River * Water system designation: [[First-class water system]], Shinano River system (designated in 1965)
== Reference Images == <gallery> Karuizawa shiraito-no-taki04s3872.jpg|{{ill|Shiraito Falls (Nagano)|ja|白糸の滝 (長野県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraito Falls (Nagano)|qq=}} Sengataki Waterfall (Karuizawa).jpg|{{ill|Sengataki Falls (Karuizawa)|ja|千ヶ滝 (軽井沢町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengataki Falls (Karuizawa)|qq=}} Yu River view from Karuizawa-ohashi bridge 2.jpg|Yukawa Valley as seen from [[Karuizawa Bridge]] Yukawa Dam and lake.jpg|Yukawa Dam File:Hanazura-inari-jinja structure 1.jpg|Area near [[Hanazura Inari Shrine]] </gallery>
== Notes == {{Citation help}} {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * [[Karuizawa Bridge]] * [[Dorokawa River]] {{Shinano River system}}

'''Yukawa''' (湯川) is a [[:en:River|River]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], flowing through [[Karuizawa Town]], [[Miyota Town]], and [[Saku City]]. It is a [[first-class river]] in the [[:en:Shinano River|Shinano River]] system. During the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], it was also known as Uogawa, Ōgawa, and Hanazugawa.<ref>''Nakasendō Volume 7'', Tokyo Bijutsu, September 30, 1979, p. 10.</ref>
== Geography ==
Yukawa originates near the northern part of [[Asama Volcano]] in [[Karuizawa Town]], Nagano Prefecture. It flows through notable locations like {{ill|Shiraito Falls (Nagano)|ja|白糸の滝 (長野県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraito Falls (Nagano)|1=Shiraito Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580293}}, {{ill|Sengataki Falls (Karuizawa)|ja|千ヶ滝 (軽井沢町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengataki Falls (Karuizawa)|1=Sengataki Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405218}}, and [[:en:Usui Pass|Usui Pass]], joining the Chikuma River (the local name for the Shinano River) at Naruse in Saku City. In Miyota Town, the Yukawa Valley houses the [[:en:Yukawa Dam|Yukawa Dam]], a prefectural [[flood control dam]] managed by Nagano Prefecture.
== Administrative Designation ==
The river has the following designations:<ref>Cabinet Order No. 43, March 24, 1965: "Ordinance Specifying River Systems and First-Class Rivers Under Article 4, Paragraph 1 of the River Act."</ref> * River designation: First-class river (designated in [[:en:1965|1965]]) * Endpoints: ** Upstream: Nagakura, Karuizawa Town, Nagano Prefecture, at the Shishiiwa land plot ** Downstream: Confluence with the Shinano River * Water system designation: [[First-class water system]], Shinano River system (designated in 1965)
== Reference Images == <gallery> Karuizawa shiraito-no-taki04s3872.jpg|{{ill|Shiraito Falls (Nagano)|ja|白糸の滝 (長野県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraito Falls (Nagano)|qq=}} Sengataki Waterfall (Karuizawa).jpg|{{ill|Sengataki Falls (Karuizawa)|ja|千ヶ滝 (軽井沢町)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengataki Falls (Karuizawa)|qq=}} Yu River view from Karuizawa-ohashi bridge 2.jpg|Yukawa Valley as seen from [[Karuizawa Bridge]] Yukawa Dam and lake.jpg|Yukawa Dam File:Hanazura-inari-jinja structure 1.jpg|Area near [[Hanazura Inari Shrine]] </gallery>

'''Yukawa''' (湯川) is a [[:en:River|River]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], flowing through [[Karuizawa Town]], [[Miyota Town]], and [[Saku City]]. It is a [[first-class river]] in the [[:en:Shinano River|Shinano River]] system. During the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], it was also known as Uogawa, Ōgawa, and Hanazugawa.<ref>''Nakasendō Volume 7'', Tokyo Bijutsu, September 30, 1979, p. 10.</ref>
== Geography ==
Yukawa originates near the northern part of [[Asama Volcano]] in [[Karuizawa Town]], Nagano Prefecture. It flows through notable locations like {{ill|Shiraito Falls (Nagano)|ja|白糸の滝 (長野県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraito Falls (Nagano)|1=Shiraito Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580293}}, {{ill|Sengataki Falls (Karuizawa)|ja|千ヶ滝 (軽井沢町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengataki Falls (Karuizawa)|1=Sengataki Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405218}}, and [[:en:Usui Pass|Usui Pass]], joining the Chikuma River (the local name for the Shinano River) at Naruse in Saku City. In Miyota Town, the Yukawa Valley houses the [[:en:Yukawa Dam|Yukawa Dam]], a prefectural [[flood control dam]] managed by Nagano Prefecture.
== Administrative Designation ==
The river has the following designations:<ref>Cabinet Order No. 43, March 24, 1965: "Ordinance Specifying River Systems and First-Class Rivers Under Article 4, Paragraph 1 of the River Act."</ref> * River designation: First-class river (designated in [[:en:1965|1965]]) * Endpoints: ** Upstream: Nagakura, Karuizawa Town, Nagano Prefecture, at the Shishiiwa land plot ** Downstream: Confluence with the Shinano River * Water system designation: [[First-class water system]], Shinano River system (designated in 1965)
== Reference Images == <gallery> Karuizawa shiraito-no-taki04s3872.jpg|{{ill|Shiraito Falls (Nagano)|ja|白糸の滝 (長野県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraito Falls (Nagano)|qq=}} Sengataki Waterfall (Karuizawa).jpg|{{ill|Sengataki Falls (Karuizawa)|ja|千ヶ滝 (軽井沢町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengataki Falls (Karuizawa)|qq=}} Yu River view from Karuizawa-ohashi bridge 2.jpg|Yukawa Valley as seen from [[Karuizawa Bridge]] Yukawa Dam and lake.jpg|Yukawa Dam File:Hanazura-inari-jinja structure 1.jpg|Area near [[Hanazura Inari Shrine]] </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yu River (Kitasaku District, Nagano)]][[ja:湯川 (北佐久郡)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11563522]] Wikidata name: Yu River [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox river | name = Yukawa | image = [[File:Yukawa Riv.JPG|250px|Taken on May 4, 2010]] | image_caption = Yukawa flowing near Nagakura Park (Asama Volcano in the background) | river_system = [[First-class river system]] | water_system = [[:en:Shinano River|Shinano River]] | river_class = [[First-class river]] | length = | source_altitude = | flow_rate = | basin_size = | source = [[Asama Volcano]] | mouth = [[:en:Shinano River|Shinano River]] (Chikuma River) | route = [[Karuizawa Town]], [[Miyota Town]], [[Saku City]], Nagano Prefecture | basin = Karuizawa Town, Miyota Town, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture | map = {{Maplink2|zoom=10|frame=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=292|frame-height=300|frame-latitude=36.27|frame-longitude=138.52 |type=line|stroke-color=#0000ff|stroke-width=3|title=Yukawa |type2=line|stroke-color2=#777777|stroke-width2=3|id2=Q734455|title2=Shinano River |text='''Blue:''' Yukawa, '''Gray:''' Shinano River (Chikuma River) }} | notes = | sources = }}
'''Yukawa''' (湯川) is a [[:en:River|River]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], flowing through [[Karuizawa Town]], [[Miyota Town]], and [[Saku City]]. It is a [[first-class river]] in the [[:en:Shinano River|Shinano River]] system. During the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], it was also known as Uogawa, Ōgawa, and Hanazugawa.<ref>''Nakasendō Volume 7'', Tokyo Bijutsu, September 30, 1979, p. 10.</ref>
== Geography ==
Yukawa originates near the northern part of [[Asama Volcano]] in [[Karuizawa Town]], Nagano Prefecture. It flows through notable locations like {{ill|Shiraito Falls (Nagano)|ja|白糸の滝 (長野県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraito Falls (Nagano)|1=Shiraito Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580293}}, {{ill|Sengataki Falls (Karuizawa)|ja|千ヶ滝 (軽井沢町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengataki Falls (Karuizawa)|1=Sengataki Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405218}}, and [[:en:Usui Pass|Usui Pass]], joining the Chikuma River (the local name for the Shinano River) at Naruse in Saku City. In Miyota Town, the Yukawa Valley houses the [[:en:Yukawa Dam|Yukawa Dam]], a prefectural [[flood control dam]] managed by Nagano Prefecture.
== Administrative Designation ==
The river has the following designations:<ref>Cabinet Order No. 43, March 24, 1965: "Ordinance Specifying River Systems and First-Class Rivers Under Article 4, Paragraph 1 of the River Act."</ref> * River designation: First-class river (designated in [[:en:1965|1965]]) * Endpoints: ** Upstream: Nagakura, Karuizawa Town, Nagano Prefecture, at the Shishiiwa land plot ** Downstream: Confluence with the Shinano River * Water system designation: [[First-class water system]], Shinano River system (designated in 1965)
== Reference Images == <gallery> Karuizawa shiraito-no-taki04s3872.jpg|{{ill|Shiraito Falls (Nagano)|ja|白糸の滝 (長野県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraito Falls (Nagano)|qq=}} Sengataki Waterfall (Karuizawa).jpg|{{ill|Sengataki Falls (Karuizawa)|ja|千ヶ滝 (軽井沢町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengataki Falls (Karuizawa)|qq=}} Yu River view from Karuizawa-ohashi bridge 2.jpg|Yukawa Valley as seen from [[Karuizawa Bridge]] Yukawa Dam and lake.jpg|Yukawa Dam File:Hanazura-inari-jinja structure 1.jpg|Area near [[Hanazura Inari Shrine]] </gallery>
== Notes == {{Citation help}} {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == {{Commonscat}} * [[Karuizawa Bridge]] * [[Dorokawa River]] {{Shinano River system}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Yu River (Kitasaku District, Nagano)]][[ja:湯川 (北佐久郡)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Yu River (Kitasaku District, Nagano)]][[ja:湯川 (北佐久郡)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yu River (Kitasaku District, Nagano)]][[ja:湯川 (北佐久郡)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11563522]] Wikidata name: Yu River [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox river | name = Yukawa | image = [[File:Yukawa Riv.JPG|250px|Taken on May 4, 2010]] | image_caption = Yukawa flowing near Nagakura Park (Asama Volcano in the background) | river_system = [[First-class river system]] | water_system = [[:en:Shinano River|Shinano River]] | river_class = [[First-class river]] | length = | source_altitude = | flow_rate = | basin_size = | source = [[Asama Volcano]] | mouth = [[:en:Shinano River|Shinano River]] (Chikuma River) | route = [[Karuizawa Town]], [[Miyota Town]], [[Saku City]], Nagano Prefecture | basin = Karuizawa Town, Miyota Town, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture | map = {{Maplink2|zoom=10|frame=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=292|frame-height=300|frame-latitude=36.27|frame-longitude=138.52 |type=line|stroke-color=#0000ff|stroke-width=3|title=Yukawa |type2=line|stroke-color2=#777777|stroke-width2=3|id2=Q734455|title2=Shinano River |text='''Blue:''' Yukawa, '''Gray:''' Shinano River (Chikuma River) }} | notes = | sources = }}
'''Yukawa''' (湯川) is a [[:en:River|River]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], flowing through [[Karuizawa Town]], [[Miyota Town]], and [[Saku City]]. It is a [[first-class river]] in the [[:en:Shinano River|Shinano River]] system. During the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], it was also known as Uogawa, Ōgawa, and Hanazugawa.<ref>''Nakasendō Volume 7'', Tokyo Bijutsu, September 30, 1979, p. 10.</ref>
== Geography ==
Yukawa originates near the northern part of [[Asama Volcano]] in [[Karuizawa Town]], Nagano Prefecture. It flows through notable locations like {{ill|Shiraito Falls (Nagano)|ja|白糸の滝 (長野県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraito Falls (Nagano)|1=Shiraito Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580293}}, {{ill|Sengataki Falls (Karuizawa)|ja|千ヶ滝 (軽井沢町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengataki Falls (Karuizawa)|1=Sengataki Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405218}}, and [[:en:Usui Pass|Usui Pass]], joining the Chikuma River (the local name for the Shinano River) at Naruse in Saku City. In Miyota Town, the Yukawa Valley houses the [[:en:Yukawa Dam|Yukawa Dam]], a prefectural [[flood control dam]] managed by Nagano Prefecture.
== Administrative Designation ==
The river has the following designations:<ref>Cabinet Order No. 43, March 24, 1965: "Ordinance Specifying River Systems and First-Class Rivers Under Article 4, Paragraph 1 of the River Act."</ref> * River designation: First-class river (designated in [[:en:1965|1965]]) * Endpoints: ** Upstream: Nagakura, Karuizawa Town, Nagano Prefecture, at the Shishiiwa land plot ** Downstream: Confluence with the Shinano River * Water system designation: [[First-class water system]], Shinano River system (designated in 1965)
== Reference Images == <gallery> Karuizawa shiraito-no-taki04s3872.jpg|{{ill|Shiraito Falls (Nagano)|ja|白糸の滝 (長野県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraito Falls (Nagano)|qq=}} Sengataki Waterfall (Karuizawa).jpg|{{ill|Sengataki Falls (Karuizawa)|ja|千ヶ滝 (軽井沢町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengataki Falls (Karuizawa)|qq=}} Yu River view from Karuizawa-ohashi bridge 2.jpg|Yukawa Valley as seen from [[Karuizawa Bridge]] Yukawa Dam and lake.jpg|Yukawa Dam File:Hanazura-inari-jinja structure 1.jpg|Area near [[Hanazura Inari Shrine]] </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yu River (Kitasaku District, Nagano)]][[ja:湯川 (北佐久郡)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yu River (Kitasaku District, Nagano)]][[ja:湯川 (北佐久郡)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yu River (Kitasaku District, Nagano)]][[ja:湯川 (北佐久郡)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11563522]] Wikidata name: Yu River [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox river | name = Yukawa | image = [[File:Yukawa Riv.JPG|250px|Taken on May 4, 2010]] | image_caption = Yukawa flowing near Nagakura Park (Asama Volcano in the background) | river_system = [[First-class river system]] | water_system = [[:en:Shinano River|Shinano River]] | river_class = [[First-class river]] | length = | source_altitude = | flow_rate = | basin_size = | source = [[Asama Volcano]] | mouth = [[:en:Shinano River|Shinano River]] (Chikuma River) | route = [[Karuizawa Town]], [[Miyota Town]], [[Saku City]], Nagano Prefecture | basin = Karuizawa Town, Miyota Town, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture | map = {{Maplink2|zoom=10|frame=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=292|frame-height=300|frame-latitude=36.27|frame-longitude=138.52 |type=line|stroke-color=#0000ff|stroke-width=3|title=Yukawa |type2=line|stroke-color2=#777777|stroke-width2=3|id2=Q734455|title2=Shinano River |text='''Blue:''' Yukawa, '''Gray:''' Shinano River (Chikuma River) }} | notes = | sources = }}
'''Yukawa''' (湯川) is a [[:en:River|River]] in [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], flowing through [[Karuizawa Town]], [[Miyota Town]], and [[Saku City]]. It is a [[first-class river]] in the [[:en:Shinano River|Shinano River]] system. During the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], it was also known as Uogawa, Ōgawa, and Hanazugawa.<ref>''Nakasendō Volume 7'', Tokyo Bijutsu, September 30, 1979, p. 10.</ref>
== Geography ==
Yukawa originates near the northern part of [[Asama Volcano]] in [[Karuizawa Town]], Nagano Prefecture. It flows through notable locations like {{ill|Shiraito Falls (Nagano)|ja|白糸の滝 (長野県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraito Falls (Nagano)|1=Shiraito Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580293}}, {{ill|Sengataki Falls (Karuizawa)|ja|千ヶ滝 (軽井沢町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengataki Falls (Karuizawa)|1=Sengataki Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405218}}, and [[:en:Usui Pass|Usui Pass]], joining the Chikuma River (the local name for the Shinano River) at Naruse in Saku City. In Miyota Town, the Yukawa Valley houses the [[:en:Yukawa Dam|Yukawa Dam]], a prefectural [[flood control dam]] managed by Nagano Prefecture.
== Administrative Designation ==
The river has the following designations:<ref>Cabinet Order No. 43, March 24, 1965: "Ordinance Specifying River Systems and First-Class Rivers Under Article 4, Paragraph 1 of the River Act."</ref> * River designation: First-class river (designated in [[:en:1965|1965]]) * Endpoints: ** Upstream: Nagakura, Karuizawa Town, Nagano Prefecture, at the Shishiiwa land plot ** Downstream: Confluence with the Shinano River * Water system designation: [[First-class water system]], Shinano River system (designated in 1965)
== Reference Images == <gallery> Karuizawa shiraito-no-taki04s3872.jpg|{{ill|Shiraito Falls (Nagano)|ja|白糸の滝 (長野県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraito Falls (Nagano)|qq=}} Sengataki Waterfall (Karuizawa).jpg|{{ill|Sengataki Falls (Karuizawa)|ja|千ヶ滝 (軽井沢町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengataki Falls (Karuizawa)|qq=}} Yu River view from Karuizawa-ohashi bridge 2.jpg|Yukawa Valley as seen from [[Karuizawa Bridge]] Yukawa Dam and lake.jpg|Yukawa Dam File:Hanazura-inari-jinja structure 1.jpg|Area near [[Hanazura Inari Shrine]] </gallery>

'''Yukawa''' (湯川) is a {{ill|River|en|River|WD=Q4022|lt=River}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}, flowing through [[Karuizawa Town]], [[Miyota Town]], and [[Saku City]]. It is a [[first-class river]] in the {{ill|Shinano River|en|Shinano River|WD=Q734455|lt=Shinano River}} system. During the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], it was also known as Uogawa, Ōgawa, and Hanazugawa.<ref>''Nakasendō Volume 7'', Tokyo Bijutsu, September 30, 1979, p. 10.</ref>
== Geography ==
Yukawa originates near the northern part of [[Asama Volcano]] in [[Karuizawa Town]], Nagano Prefecture. It flows through notable locations like {{ill|Shiraito Falls (Nagano)|ja|白糸の滝 (長野県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraito Falls (Nagano)|1=Shiraito Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580293}}, {{ill|Sengataki Falls (Karuizawa)|ja|千ヶ滝 (軽井沢町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengataki Falls (Karuizawa)|1=Sengataki Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405218}}, and {{ill|Usui Pass|en|Usui Pass|WD=Q847860|lt=Usui Pass}}, joining the Chikuma River (the local name for the Shinano River) at Naruse in Saku City. In Miyota Town, the Yukawa Valley houses the {{ill|Yukawa Dam|en|Yukawa Dam|WD=Q8060602|lt=Yukawa Dam}}, a prefectural [[flood control dam]] managed by Nagano Prefecture.
== Administrative Designation ==
The river has the following designations:<ref>Cabinet Order No. 43, March 24, 1965: "Ordinance Specifying River Systems and First-Class Rivers Under Article 4, Paragraph 1 of the River Act."</ref> * River designation: First-class river (designated in {{ill|1965|en|1965|WD=Q2650|lt=1965}}) * Endpoints: ** Upstream: Nagakura, Karuizawa Town, Nagano Prefecture, at the Shishiiwa land plot ** Downstream: Confluence with the Shinano River * Water system designation: [[First-class water system]], Shinano River system (designated in 1965)

== Geography ==
Yukawa originates near the northern part of [[Asama Volcano]] in [[Karuizawa Town]], Nagano Prefecture. It flows through notable locations like {{ill|Shiraito Falls (Nagano)|ja|白糸の滝 (長野県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraito Falls (Nagano)|1=Shiraito Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580293}}, {{ill|Sengataki Falls (Karuizawa)|ja|千ヶ滝 (軽井沢町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengataki Falls (Karuizawa)|1=Sengataki Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405218}}, and {{ill|Usui Pass|en|Usui Pass|WD=Q847860|lt=Usui Pass|1=Usui Pass}}, joining the Chikuma River (the local name for the Shinano River) at Naruse in Saku City. In Miyota Town, the Yukawa Valley houses the {{ill|Yukawa Dam|en|Yukawa Dam|WD=Q8060602|lt=Yukawa Dam|1=Yukawa Dam}}, a prefectural [[flood control dam]] managed by Nagano Prefecture.
== Administrative Designation ==
The river has the following designations:<ref>Cabinet Order No. 43, March 24, 1965: "Ordinance Specifying River Systems and First-Class Rivers Under Article 4, Paragraph 1 of the River Act."</ref> * River designation: First-class river (designated in {{ill|1965|en|1965|WD=Q2650|lt=1965|1=1965}}) * Endpoints: ** Upstream: Nagakura, Karuizawa Town, Nagano Prefecture, at the Shishiiwa land plot ** Downstream: Confluence with the Shinano River * Water system designation: [[First-class water system]], Shinano River system (designated in 1965)

{{Infobox river | name = Yukawa | image = [[File:Yukawa Riv.JPG|250px|Taken on May 4, 2010]] | image_caption = Yukawa flowing near Nagakura Park (Asama Volcano in the background) | river_system = [[First-class river system]] | water_system = {{ill|Shinano River|en|Shinano River|WD=Q734455|lt=Shinano River|1=Shinano River}} | river_class = [[First-class river]] | length = | source_altitude = | flow_rate = | basin_size = | source = [[Asama Volcano]] | mouth = {{ill|Shinano River|en|Shinano River|WD=Q734455|lt=Shinano River|1=Shinano River}} (Chikuma River) | route = [[Karuizawa Town]], [[Miyota Town]], [[Saku City]], Nagano Prefecture | basin = Karuizawa Town, Miyota Town, Saku City, Nagano Prefecture | map = {{Maplink2|zoom=10|frame=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=292|frame-height=300|frame-latitude=36.27|frame-longitude=138.52 |type=line|stroke-color=#0000ff|stroke-width=3|title=Yukawa |type2=line|stroke-color2=#777777|stroke-width2=3|id2=Q734455|title2=Shinano River |text='''Blue:''' Yukawa, '''Gray:''' Shinano River (Chikuma River) }} | notes = | sources = }}
'''Yukawa''' (湯川) is a {{ill|River|en|River|WD=Q4022|lt=River|1=River}} in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, flowing through [[Karuizawa Town]], [[Miyota Town]], and [[Saku City]]. It is a [[first-class river]] in the {{ill|Shinano River|en|Shinano River|WD=Q734455|lt=Shinano River|1=Shinano River}} system. During the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], it was also known as Uogawa, Ōgawa, and Hanazugawa.<ref>''Nakasendō Volume 7'', Tokyo Bijutsu, September 30, 1979, p. 10.</ref>
== Geography ==
Yukawa originates near the northern part of [[Asama Volcano]] in [[Karuizawa Town]], Nagano Prefecture. It flows through notable locations like {{ill|Shiraito Falls (Nagano)|ja|白糸の滝 (長野県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiraito Falls (Nagano)|1=Shiraito Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11580293}}, {{ill|Sengataki Falls (Karuizawa)|ja|千ヶ滝 (軽井沢町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengataki Falls (Karuizawa)|1=Sengataki Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405218}}, and {{ill|Usui Pass|en|Usui Pass|WD=Q847860|lt=Usui Pass|1=Usui Pass}}, joining the Chikuma River (the local name for the Shinano River) at Naruse in Saku City. In Miyota Town, the Yukawa Valley houses the {{ill|Yukawa Dam|en|Yukawa Dam|WD=Q8060602|lt=Yukawa Dam|1=Yukawa Dam}}, a prefectural [[flood control dam]] managed by Nagano Prefecture.
== Administrative Designation ==
The river has the following designations:<ref>Cabinet Order No. 43, March 24, 1965: "Ordinance Specifying River Systems and First-Class Rivers Under Article 4, Paragraph 1 of the River Act."</ref> * River designation: First-class river (designated in {{ill|1965|en|1965|WD=Q2650|lt=1965|1=1965}}) * Endpoints: ** Upstream: Nagakura, Karuizawa Town, Nagano Prefecture, at the Shishiiwa land plot ** Downstream: Confluence with the Shinano River * Water system designation: [[First-class water system]], Shinano River system (designated in 1965)
